op_000002.source_statement: ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv B A) ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Γ ++ [A]) B
op_000002.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv B A) ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Γ ++ [A]) B
op_000002.statement_formalization_notes: Replaced the infix `⧸` with the fully qualified constructor `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv` to avoid parser resolution against quotient notation outside the namespace.

op_000002.proof_sketch: Use the existing invertibility theorem for the forward implication and the right-division introduction rule for the reverse implication. From a derivation of `Sequent Γ (Tp.rdiv B A)`, `nonempty_premises` gives `Γ ≠ []` and `rdiv_invertible` gives `Sequent (Γ ++ [A]) B`. Conversely, from `Γ ≠ []` and `Sequent (Γ ++ [A]) B`, apply `Sequent.rdiv_r`.
op_000002.proof_text:
constructor
· intro h
  constructor
  · exact Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.nonempty_premises h
  · simpa using Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.rdiv_invertible h
· rintro ⟨hΓ, h⟩
  exact Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.rdiv_r hΓ h

op_000002.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.002.lean:11:2: error: Tactic `constructor` failed: target is not an inductive datatype | ⊢ ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, | Γ ⇒ B.rdiv A ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ Γ ++ [A] ⇒ B

op_000002.proof_sketch: The previous proof failed because the goal was still a universally quantified statement, so `constructor` was applied too early. First introduce the implicit parameters `Γ`, `A`, and `B`. Then prove the forward direction using `nonempty_premises` and `rdiv_invertible`, and the reverse direction using the constructor `Sequent.rdiv_r`.
op_000002.proof_text:
intro Γ A B
constructor
· intro h
  constructor
  · exact Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.nonempty_premises h
  · simpa using Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.rdiv_invertible h
· rintro ⟨hΓ, h⟩
  exact Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.rdiv_r hΓ h

op_000001.source_statement: ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv A B) ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (A :: Γ) B
op_000001.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv A B) ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (A :: Γ) B
op_000001.statement_formalization_notes: Replaced the local `⧹` notation with the fully qualified constructor `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv` to avoid parser dependence on namespace-local notation.

op_000001.proof_sketch: Use the existing left-division inversion theorem for the forward direction, together with nonemptiness of premises; for the reverse direction, apply the `Sequent.ldiv_r` constructor after rewriting `A :: Γ` as `[A] ++ Γ`.
op_000001.proof_text:
intro Γ A B
  constructor
  · intro h
    refine ⟨Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.nonempty_premises h, ?_⟩
    simpa using Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.ldiv_invertible h
  · rintro ⟨hΓ, h⟩
    exact Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.ldiv_r hΓ (by simpa using h)
